content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Project Template for Debugging
content.seo_description: Stop struggling to debug code. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to automate complex troubleshooting and ship client projects faster.
content.answer_block: The 'can't debug code' bottleneck stems from context fragmentation. By integrating the Antigravity project template, you establish a structured, AI-readable codebase architecture. When paired with Claude Code, you gain an autonomous debugging engine that maps dependencies, isolates logic errors, and executes precision fixes, effectively slashing your technical debt and development time.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a screen at 2 AM, the client’s deadline is looming, and a cryptic stack trace has turned your codebase into a labyrinth of errors. You know the logic is sound, yet the execution fails, leaving you paralyzed by the sheer volume of noise. This isn't just a bug; it's a productivity black hole that threatens your reputation as a high-velocity freelancer.

But what if you could offload the cognitive heavy lifting of root-cause analysis to a system designed for precision? Enter the Antigravity project template—a robust, modular framework that treats your codebase as an AI-navigable graph. By combining this architectural rigor with Claude Code's autonomous reasoning, you transform debugging from an exhausting investigative chore into a surgical, repeatable process.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Standardize your project structure to ensure AI agents have clear visibility into your dependency graph and modular logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the Antigravity template for a TypeScript-based SaaS, ensuring strict separation of concerns and a centralized error handling middleware.

steps.title: Contextualize the Bug
steps.description: Feed the specific error trace and surrounding module context into the Antigravity environment to limit the AI's search space.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current error trace in /src/services/auth.ts. Map the dependency chain of the failing function and identify potential state mismatches.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --context ./src/services/auth.ts --task 'identify root cause of auth middleware failure'

steps.title: Automated Isolation
steps.description: Use isolation patterns to force the code into a failing state, allowing the AI to verify the fix without affecting production modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a sandbox test file that isolates the failing function, mocking external API dependencies to force the error reproduction.

steps.title: Precision Refactoring
steps.description: Apply the fix while maintaining the structural integrity defined by the Antigravity template.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply a hotfix to the auth module that handles the null pointer exception while strictly adhering to the existing project architecture and type safety rules.

steps.title: Regression Validation
steps.description: Run a full suite of automated checks to ensure the fix hasn't introduced side effects in interconnected modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Execute a full regression suite across the entire project structure. Report any unexpected behavioral shifts in downstream services.

pro_tips: Use Antigravity's 'trace-map' directive to force the AI to visualize the data flow before suggesting any code changes.
Always set your Claude Code 'temperature' to 0.1 during debugging to ensure deterministic, logical output rather than creative hallucination.
Maintain a 'knowledge-base.md' file in your root; Claude Code will automatically ingest this to understand your specific client-side business logic.
When debugging state-heavy apps, use the 'snapshot' feature to capture the exact memory state of the app before and after the bug occurs.
Create custom 'shortcut scripts' in your package.json that invoke Claude Code with your most common debugging prompts to save time.
resources: Best Practices for AI Engineering: https://ai-builder.dev/patterns
content.next_steps: After completing your first debug cycle, integrate the 'Antigravity' monitoring hook to receive proactive alerts before bugs reach your client's production environment.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start orchestrating it with the power of Antigravity and Claude Code. Elevate your freelance workflow, deliver cleaner builds, and reclaim your time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
